content.problemStatement:
Discuss data exploration and visualization techniques using Pandas, Matplotlib, and
Seaborn.---3m
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a space‑separated list of integers representing the data set for that test case. The number of integers N in a line satisfies 1 ≤ N ≤ 1000 and each integer is between 1 and 1000.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output exactly the line:
Histogram, Box Plot, Scatter Plot

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. The program reads the five numbers, ignores them, and prints the required placeholder.
Again a single test case; regardless of the actual values the output is the fixed placeholder.
The same placeholder is printed for any valid data set.

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput:
Histogram, Box Plot, Scatter Plot
Histogram, Box Plot, Scatter Plot

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Histogram, Box Plot, Scatter Plot
Histogram, Box Plot, Scatter Plot
Histogram, Box Plot, Scatter Plot

solution.approach: The task does not require any actual plotting. For each test case we only need to read the whole line (the numbers can be ignored) and print the fixed placeholder text. The algorithm is: read T, loop T times, read a line with fgets/getline/BufferedReader/input/fs, and output the constant string. This satisfies the judge which expects exactly that text for every valid input.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of input tokens) – we read each integer once, but the processing per integer is O(1). The dominant work is I/O.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) where L is the length of the longest input line (at most a few thousand characters). No additional large structures are used.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    T = int(data[0].strip())
    out_lines = []
    for i in range(1, 1 + T):
        # each line data[i] contains the numbers, but we ignore them
        out_lines.append('Histogram, Box Plot, Scatter Plot')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
